children.content: Equal parts Campari and sweet vermouth, topped with soda. Italian aperitif served in Schumann's Munich since 1982.
[4] Lower-ABV Negroni cousin; Schumann's house aperitif
Equal parts Campari and sweet vermouth, topped with soda. Italian aperitif served in Schumann's Munich since 1982.
3 parts prosecco, 2 parts Aperol, 1 part soda water, served over ice in a wine glass. Italian aperitivo standard.
[5] 3-2-1 formula; fastest party drink to batch
3 parts prosecco, 2 parts Aperol, 1 part soda water, served over ice in a wine glass. Italian aperitivo standard.
Prosecco, white peach purée. Invented at Harry's Bar in Venice, 1948.
Prosecco, white peach purée. Invented at Harry's Bar in Venice, 1948.
Vodka, tomato juice, lemon, Worcestershire, Tabasco, salt and pepper. The brunch heavyweight.

Vodka, tomato juice, lemon, Worcestershire, Tabasco, salt and pepper. The brunch heavyweight.
Cachaça, fresh lime quartered and muddled with sugar, served over crushed ice. Brazil's national cocktail.
[4] Brazil's national; muddle in glass per drink
Cachaça, fresh lime quartered and muddled with sugar, served over crushed ice. Brazil's national cocktail.
Citrus vodka, triple sec, cranberry juice, fresh lime, shaken and served in a chilled coupe. Toby Cecchini, 1988.
Citrus vodka, triple sec, cranberry juice, fresh lime, shaken and served in a chilled coupe. Toby Cecchini, 1988.
White rum, fresh lime juice, simple syrup, shaken hard with ice and double-strained. The platonic ideal of a sour.
White rum, fresh lime juice, simple syrup, shaken hard with ice and double-strained. The platonic ideal of a sour.
Dark rum poured over ice, topped with spicy ginger beer, lime squeeze. Bermuda's national drink.
Dark rum poured over ice, topped with spicy ginger beer, lime squeeze. Bermuda's national drink.
Vodka, coffee liqueur, fresh espresso, simple syrup. Shaken hard for the signature crema. Dick Bradsell, 1983.
[5] Modern classic; needs fresh espresso per drink
Vodka, coffee liqueur, fresh espresso, simple syrup. Shaken hard for the signature crema. Dick Bradsell, 1983.
Gin, fresh lemon, sugar, topped with champagne. Named for the WWI 75mm field gun. Best as a welcome cocktail.
Gin, fresh lemon, sugar, topped with champagne. Named for the WWI 75mm field gun. Best as a welcome cocktail.
Vodka, Chambord black raspberry liqueur, pineapple juice. The signature foam comes from hard-shaken pineapple.
Vodka, Chambord black raspberry liqueur, pineapple juice. The signature foam comes from hard-shaken pineapple.
Gin, fresh lemon, sugar, topped with soda. The simplest of the fizz family.
Gin, fresh lemon, sugar, topped with soda. The simplest of the fizz family.
Prosecco, elderflower liqueur, soda, fresh mint and lime. South Tyrol original, now a global aperitivo.
Prosecco, elderflower liqueur, soda, fresh mint and lime. South Tyrol original, now a global aperitivo.
Dark rum, Campari, pineapple, lime, sugar. 1978 Kuala Lumpur Hilton original; rediscovered by NYC bartenders 2010s.

Dark rum, Campari, pineapple, lime, sugar. 1978 Kuala Lumpur Hilton original; rediscovered by NYC bartenders 2010s.
Champagne with a splash of crème de cassis. Bourgogne classic.

Champagne with a splash of crème de cassis. Bourgogne classic.
Equal parts vodka, gin, rum, tequila, triple sec, lemon, splash of cola. Looks like iced tea, hits like a brick.

Equal parts vodka, gin, rum, tequila, triple sec, lemon, splash of cola. Looks like iced tea, hits like a brick.
Aged rum, fresh lime, orange curaçao, orgeat almond syrup. Trader Vic's 1944 original — not a fruit punch.

Aged rum, fresh lime, orange curaçao, orgeat almond syrup. Trader Vic's 1944 original — not a fruit punch.
Rye whiskey, sweet vermouth, Angostura bitters, stirred over ice. Garnished with a brandied cherry.

Rye whiskey, sweet vermouth, Angostura bitters, stirred over ice. Garnished with a brandied cherry.
Tequila, triple sec, fresh lime juice, shaken with ice and served in a salt-rimmed glass. The world's most-ordered cocktail.
Tequila, triple sec, fresh lime juice, shaken with ice and served in a salt-rimmed glass. The world's most-ordered cocktail.
Equal parts champagne and fresh orange juice. The brunch standard since 1925.
Equal parts champagne and fresh orange juice. The brunch standard since 1925.
Bourbon, sugar, mint, crushed ice in a copper cup. Mint gently muddled, never bruised.

Bourbon, sugar, mint, crushed ice in a copper cup. Mint gently muddled, never bruised.
White rum, lime juice, sugar, fresh mint leaves muddled, topped with soda water and ice. Classic Cuban highball.

White rum, lime juice, sugar, fresh mint leaves muddled, topped with soda water and ice. Classic Cuban highball.
Vodka, ginger beer, fresh lime in a copper mug. Mid-century American invention to push vodka and ginger beer.

Vodka, ginger beer, fresh lime in a copper mug. Mid-century American invention to push vodka and ginger beer.
Equal parts mezcal, yellow Chartreuse, Aperol, fresh lime. Joaquín Simó at Death & Co, 2011.

Equal parts gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth, stirred and served over ice with an orange peel. The most batch-friendly classic.

Sugar muddled with Angostura bitters and a splash of water, then bourbon stirred over ice. The benchmark spirit-forward cocktail.

Tequila, grapefruit soda (Squirt or Jarritos), fresh lime, salt rim. Mexico's most-consumed tequila drink — not the margarita.
